# negatives corpus. Every section here MUST produce zero pattern matches and
# zero deep findings. Dummy values that the regexes intentionally match
# (AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE, 4111-1111-1111-1111, the canonical jwt.io token, the
# nil UUID, RFC 5737/3849 documentation IPs, RFC 2606 example domains, etc.)
# are deliberately NOT here: those are an allowlist concern, not false
# positives, and live outside this change.

# === English prose ===
The quarterly review went well and the team agreed to ship the new onboarding
flow once the remaining copy has been polished. Everyone felt the discussion
was productive and the next steps were clear to all of us in the room.

# === French prose ===
Le rapport trimestriel a ete bien recu et l equipe a decide de livrer le
nouveau parcours apres une derniere relecture du texte. La discussion fut
utile et chacun est reparti avec une idee claire des prochaines etapes.

# === Italian prose ===
La revisione trimestrale e andata bene e il gruppo ha deciso di rilasciare il
nuovo percorso dopo un ultimo controllo dei testi. La conversazione e stata
utile e tutti hanno lasciato la sala con le idee chiare sui passi successivi.

# === Spanish prose ===
La revision trimestral salio bien y el equipo acordo lanzar el nuevo flujo
despues de pulir los textos restantes. La conversacion resulto util y todos
salimos de la sala con una idea clara de los siguientes pasos a seguir.

# === Below-threshold key value pairs ===
key=ab12
code=12ab
pin=4u7
ratio=hi

# === Out of context short numbers ===
We sold 12345 widgets and shipped order 4567 the same afternoon.
The recipe needs 350 grams of flour and about 12 minutes in the oven.

# === Placeholder tokens ===
Use xxx or *** as a stand-in here, and replace changeme before launch.
The config still says your-api-key-here and TODO in a couple of spots.

# === BIP39 wordlist run below the twelve-word threshold ===
# Exactly 11 wordlist words, isolated by numeric-only tokens (which normalize
# to empty and break the run), so the 12-word mnemonic floor is not reached.
1234567
legal winner thank year wave sausage worth useful legal winner thank
7654321

# === Vendor host without a token ===
# The vendor-host heuristic needs both the anchor and an in-window token; a
# bare host on its own must stay quiet.
We host the public API at api.linode.com and keep the guide online for now.
Send the X-Api-Key header but omit the value here while we draft the docs.

# === Token without a vendor host ===
# A bare opaque token with no host/label anchor nearby must stay quiet.
A standalone marker abcdEFghijklMNopqrst sits in this note without any anchor.

# === Vendor brand names in prose ===
We evaluated Datadog, New Relic, Linode, Hetzner, Neon, Vultr, Scaleway and
Postmark before settling on the current monitoring and hosting stack we run.

# === Near-miss vendor token prefixes ===
# Prefixes are present but the body is too short to clear the length floor, so
# the backfilled SecretPatterns must not fire.
The secret_value and lin_api_key notes are placeholders, not real tokens.
A packagist_uat_ entry with no digest and an empty ntn_ marker stay quiet.

# === Block 1 precision-fix near misses ===
# Old over-broad Azure Entra shape (no offset-4 \dQ~ anchor) must stay quiet
# now that the pattern was tightened.
Bb2~Cc3Dd4Ee5Ff6Gg7Hh8Ii9Jj0Kk1Ll2Mm3N
# ghs_ below the 36-char floor, ABSK below the 109-char floor, and an
# Atlassian token missing the fixed 3xFfG segment are all non-tokens.
ghs_tooShort and ABSKtooshort plus ATATT3plainTokenNoMagicSeg here
# sk- followed by 47 chars (one short of the classic 48) and no T3BlbkFJ.
sk-abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRST

# === Block 3a prefix near misses ===
# Each prefix is present but the body is below its length floor, so the new
# Block 3a SecretPatterns must not fire. Tokens are deliberately short to
# avoid the broad low-severity catch-alls (MAC, Solana base58, card, phone).
vcp_tiny, sk-do-x, dp.audit.x, console-x, fc-dead, sbp_abc, sk_abcdef,
apiv3_x, lsv2_pt_x, pscale_tkn_x, ek_x and pcsk_a_b all stay quiet.

# === Block 3b prefix near misses ===
# Prefixes present, body below floor; short on purpose (see Block 3a note).
secret-test-x, project-live-x, ory_pat_x, ramp_sec_x, hxtp_x, pnu_x,
endr+x, sktsec_x, duffel_live_x, ddw_x and rh-api-x all stay quiet.

# === Block 3c prefix near misses ===
# Prefixes present, body below floor; short on purpose (see Block 3a note).
cfk_x, nbt_x, nbp_x, dnkey-x, pk1_x, AuthKey_x.p8, sk_appl_x, ek_test_x,
pdl_live_x, A21AA-x, APP_USR-x, KEYshort and a .access marker stay quiet.

# === Block 3d hash family near misses ===
# Tag/brace present but no payload, or below the length floor; short on
# purpose (see Block 3a note).
{SHA} and {CRYPT} bare, $keepass$ alone, WPA*0 fragment, $A$0$ short,
pbkdf2_sha256$ with no salt, grub.pbkdf2.sha512. truncated stay quiet.

# === Block 3e auth near misses ===
# Cookie/param names present but value below floor; short on purpose. Header
# labels are intentionally omitted: a pre-existing deep_scan heuristic
# ("Generic API Key Header") legitimately flags label+nearby-token, which is
# unrelated to these Block 3e regex patterns.
sessionid=x, session=a.b.c, code_verifier=short, auth_req_id=x and a bare
__VIEWSTATE=short marker all stay quiet.

# === Block 4a MRZ / credential-file near misses ===
# A TD3-shaped MRZ whose document-number check digit is wrong (6 -> 0):
# the mrz_td3 validator must reject it, so nothing fires.
P<UTOERIKSSON<<ANNA<MARIA<<<<<<<<<<<<<<<<<<<
L898902C30UTO7408122F1204159ZE184226B<<<<<10
# pgpass-shaped line with a trivially short password tail stays quiet.
host.example.com:5432:db:user:x

# === Block 4b driver-license near misses ===
# Too short / wrong shape for the distinctive DL formats; numeric-only and
# CA-short DL forms are intentionally kw-gated (deferred to Block 7), so a
# CA-short A+7-digit string must NOT fire here.
A1234567 and AB12345 and A1B2C3 all stay quiet.

# === Block 5 IBAN checksum gate ===
# IBAN-shaped but ISO 7064 MOD 97-10 invalid (NL prefix, not a per-country
# fast-path): the iban_mod97 validator must reject it so nothing fires.
NL00ABNAXYZ1A2B3C4D5E is not a valid IBAN here.

# === Block 6a EU gov-ID near misses ===
# Labels present but the number is too short to match the structure (and
# checksum-invalid forms are unit-tested in validators::eu_govid_known_
# vectors). Kept free of long digit runs to avoid the phone/card catch-alls.
PESEL: 123, BSN: 45, RRN: 6 and NIR: 789 are too short to even match.

# === Block 6b Spain DNI/NIE checksum gate ===
# Label present but the control letter is wrong: spain_dni must reject it.
# 8 digits only (no phone/card-length run) keeps the broad catch-alls quiet.
DNI: 12345678A is not a valid Spanish DNI.

# === Block 6c Brazil CNPJ/CPF checksum gate ===
# 2026 alphanumeric CNPJ shape with wrong check digits: brazil_cnpj must
# reject it. Alphanumeric (letters present) so no phone/card digit-run.
12ABC34DE5FG99 is not a valid 2026 CNPJ.

# === Block 6d EU checksum-gated near misses ===
# Estonia/Czech/Romania are now keyword-free but checksum-gated; invalid
# check-digit forms are unit-tested in validators::eu_govid_known_vectors.
# These are structurally too short to match at all, with no long digit run
# so the phone/card/CPF catch-alls also stay quiet.
Isikukood 376, rodne cislo 12/34 and CNP 198 stay quiet.

# === Block 6e China Resident ID checksum gate ===
# 18-char RIC shape is now ISO 7064 MOD 11-2 gated (invalid check char
# unit-tested in validators::eu_govid_known_vectors). This is too short
# to match at all; no long digit run keeps the catch-alls quiet.
China ID 11010519 is far too short to be a RIC.

# === Block 6f SSN / IMEI / ICCID gates ===
# SSN with an invalid area (000) -> us_ssn rejects it; the imei/iccid
# labels carry sub-length bodies below the structural floor. Invalid
# Luhn/structural forms are unit-tested in validators.
SSN 000-12-3456 is invalid; imei 123 and iccid 89 are too short.

# === Block 6g MEA gov-ID gates ===
# Turkey TCKN is keyword-free + double-check-digit gated; Israel TZ is
# label-gated. Both kept structurally too short here (no 11-digit run)
# so neither fires; invalid-checksum forms are unit-tested in validators.
TC kimlik 100 and teudat zehut 12 are too short to match.

# === Block 6h NPI / DEA / NHS checksum gates ===
# DEA/NHS/NPI are now checksum-gated (DEA: dea, NHS: nhs_mod11, NPI:
# npi_80840/luhn). These are sub-length, no digit run; invalid-checksum
# forms are unit-tested in validators.
DEA AB123, NHS: 400 22 and NPI: 999 are too short to match.

# === Block 7a E.164 country-code post-filter ===
# Unassigned-CC and freephone E.164 are rejected by the e164 filter
# (unit-tested in validators). Kept too short to match the regex at all
# and free of long digit runs so the phone catch-alls also stay quiet.
Call +999 or the freephone +800 line; neither is a personal number.

# === Block 7b per-country phone NSN-length gates ===
# Numbers too short for their country's national number length are
# rejected by the per-country validators; kept short enough that the
# generic E.164 and US 3-3-4 catch-alls also stay quiet.
Call +44 12345 or +1 555 12 - both too short to be real numbers.

# === Block 8a vehicle / maritime / mail checksum gates ===
# VIN/IMO/container are checksum-gated (invalid forms unit-tested in
# validators). These are structurally too short to match at all and
# carry no long digit run, so the broad catch-alls also stay quiet.
VIN 1HGCM, IMO 90747 and container CSQU30 are too short.

# === Block 8b geo coordinate range gate ===
# A geo: URI whose lat/lon are out of range is rejected by geo_coord
# (in-range vectors are unit-tested in validators). No long digit run.
geo:999.9,-999.9 is not a valid location on Earth.

# === Block 8c device token / fingerprint near misses ===
# Structured prefixes/suffixes present but bodies below the floor, so
# the self-distinct device patterns must not fire.
A fingerprint AB:CD, a SHA256: stub and a GA1.2.1 cookie all stay quiet.

# === Block 8d genetic-file header near misses ===
# Talking about the formats without the actual headers / sequence data
# must not fire the biometric patterns.
We discussed rsid columns and a FASTA export but pasted no data here.

# === Block 11 do-NOT-redact allowlist ===
# Anti-CSRF / OAuth-public / device-flow tokens and wire-signature
# headers carry token-shaped values that would otherwise trip entropy
# or generic patterns; the built-in allowlist must suppress all of them.
csrftoken=Gx7Hk2pLqR9sT4vW8yZ1aB3cD5eF6gHjK
X-CSRF-Token: aBcDeF0123456789aBcDeF0123456789aBcD
__RequestVerificationToken=Zm9vYmFyYmF6cXV4MTIzNDU2Nzg5MGFiY2RlZg
The OAuth redirect carried state=Gx7Hk2pLqR9sT4vW8yZ1aB3cD5eF6gHjK
and nonce=aB3cD5eF6gHjK7lM9nP1qR3sT5vW7yZ9 plus
code_challenge=E9Melhoa2OwvFrEMTJguCHaoeK1t8URWbuGJSstw-cM here.
Device flow shows user_code=WDJB-MJHT for the user to type.
WWW-Authenticate: Bearer realm="api", error="invalid_token"
X-Hub-Signature-256: sha256=7d38cdd689735b008b3c702edd92eea23791c5f6
Stripe-Signature: t=1700000000,v1=5257a869e7ecebeda32affa62cdca3fa51cad7e77a0e56ff536d0ce8e108d8bd
webhook-signature: v1,g0hM9SsE+OTPJTGt/tmIKtSyZlE3uFJELVlNIOLJ1OE=

# === Block 9/10 long-tail near misses ===
# Prefixes/labels present but bodies below floor; ORCID/ISIN checksum
# forms unit-tested in validators. No long digit run.
An ORCID 0000-0002 stub, ISIN US03, an otpauth link with no secret,
and HC1: alone all stay quiet.

# === Soft-wrap guard: blank line between halves ===
# A blank line leaves both newlines in place, so no AKIA... key forms.
AKIAIOSFOD

NN7EXAMPLE

# === Soft-wrap guard: second line starts with a space ===
# Leading space after the break means it is not one wrapped token.
AKIAIOSFOD
 NN7EXAMPLE

# === Soft-wrap must not fuse ordinary wrapped prose ===
# A hard-wrapped sentence has spaces / sentence punctuation at the break,
# so it is never treated as one token.
Please review the first paragraph of the report before the meeting
and then forward the summary to the rest of the team afterwards.

# === FP lock: bare 9-digit run is not a US passport ===
# The old US Passport rule was a bare \b\d{9}\b - it redacted every order,
# zip+4 and tracking number. It is now keyword-gated, so an unlabeled run
# must stay quiet.
Order 483920114 shipped, tracking ref 729104553 attached.

# === FP lock: card shape without a valid Luhn checksum ===
# Visa/Mastercard BIN shape but the Mod-10 check fails: the card patterns
# are now luhn-gated, so a coincidental 16-digit run must not redact.
Invoice 4111 1111 1111 1112 and ticket 5555 5555 5555 4445 are internal ids.
A plain 16-digit order number 1234567890123456 is not a card.

# === FP lock: 12-hex short hash is not a bare MAC ===
# The separator-less 12-hex MAC form was removed - it redacted git short
# SHAs / hex ids. A bare 12-hex run must now stay quiet.
Build commit a1b2c3d4e5f6 deployed; blob id 0e02b2c3d479 cached.

# === FP lock: Placeholder-style sample values stay inert ===
# The Placeholder redaction style emits these as fake examples. Each is
# format-valid but fails its checksum (Amex Mod-10, IBAN Mod-97), so a
# re-scan of redacted output must not detect them.
Amex sample 3782 822463 10006 and IBAN sample DE00370400440532013000 here.
